title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple and Mrs. Elizabeth McGillicuddy plan a trip to Blakenham so that Miss Marple can visit the crime scene. They take the 4:50 p.m. train from Paddington Station on a Friday near Christmas. During the journey, a ticket inspector enters their carriage, stumbling slightly as the train navigates a long curve. Upon arriving at the crowded Blakenham station, Mrs. McGillicuddy departs to meet Margaret for tea, while Miss Marple remains on the train. As they say goodbye, Mrs. McGillicuddy recalls how Miss Marple previously helped a man named Rodrigo by solving the mystery of disappearing money from a locked cabinet.

While traveling back, Miss Marple reflects on her desire to solve the case despite her advancing age and physical frailty. She weighs her potential "assets" for an investigation, including her understanding of human nature, her connection to Sir Henry Clithering and his godson at Scotland Yard, her nephew Raymond West’s son David who works for British Railways, Leonard (Gwender’s son) who is skilled with maps, and her friend Florence. 

A sudden realization occurs to Miss Marple regarding the train's movement through a bend in the embankment, which occurs around the same time the ticket inspector had checked her ticket. This idea revitalizes her, and she decides to take action. She writes a letter to Raymond West requesting urgent information and, during a Christmas dinner at the vicarage, consults with Leonard about maps to further her plans.
